serving.natural_language_portion: One serving of Ramen Matsuri Finger Bites is approximately 150 g, typically comprising slices of braised pork belly, pork and chicken chashu, and a halved soft-boiled marinated egg, served as a starter or side dish.

nutrient_source.title: Pork belly braised and chashu-style preparations; soft-boiled egg — composite estimate from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entries: Pork, fresh, belly, raw (NDB 10006); Egg, whole, cooked, hard-boiled (NDB 01129); Chicken, broilers or fryers, meat only, cooked, roasted (NDB 05013)
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service, FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No direct entry exists for Ramen Matsuri Finger Bites in any approved primary source. Nutrient values are estimated from a weighted composite of USDA SR Legacy entries for braised pork belly (approx. 55% by weight), pork/chicken chashu (approx. 30%), and soft-boiled marinated egg (approx. 15%), adjusted for typical soy-mirin marinade sodium contribution. Values represent a reasonable per-100g estimate for this mixed meat and egg appetiser.

gi_source.title: Glycemic index of mixed meat and egg dishes — analogous estimate based on braised pork and egg preparations; reference: Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC. International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008. Diabetes Care. 2008;31(12):2281-2283.
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care peer-reviewed journal
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Ramen Matsuri Finger Bites. GI estimated at 32 based on analogous high-protein, high-fat, low-carbohydrate meat and egg dishes from the University of Sydney GI Database and Atkinson et al. 2008 international GI tables. The dish is predominantly protein and fat with minimal carbohydrate (2.8 g per 100g from soy-mirin marinade), consistent with a low GI. Glycemic load per 150g serving = (32 x 4.2g available carbohydrate) / 100 = 1.3.

hypertension.key_factor: High sodium content from soy sauce and mirin marinade (~520 mg per 100g, ~780 mg per serving)
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit or avoid this dish due to its high sodium content from soy-based marinades; a single 150g serving provides approximately 780 mg sodium, representing over one-third of the recommended daily limit of 2000 mg.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Low carbohydrate content and low glycemic index (GI 32) with moderate protein and fat
type2_diabetes.guidance: Ramen Matsuri Finger Bites have a low glycemic index of approximately 32 and a glycemic load of only 1.3 per serving, making them suitable for blood glucose management in individuals with Type 2 Diabetes, though the high sodium and saturated fat content warrant attention to overall dietary balance.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Elevated saturated fat from pork belly and high sodium from soy marinade
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: The combination of saturated fat (~5.8 g per 100g) from pork belly and high sodium (~520 mg per 100g) in this dish poses a moderate cardiovascular risk; individuals with cardiovascular disease should consume this dish occasionally and in small portions, prioritising leaner protein alternatives.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Ramen Matsuri Finger Bites is a Japanese-style appetiser or snack dish served at Ramen Matsuri restaurant in Singapore. The dish features a curated selection of ramen toppings presented as finger food, typically comprising slices of braised pork belly (kakuni-style), pork chashu, chicken chashu, and a halved soft-boiled marinated egg (ajitsuke tamago). The pork belly is slow-braised in a soy sauce, mirin, sake, and sugar mixture until tender and richly flavoured, while the chashu is rolled and roasted or braised pork or chicken that is sliced thinly. The soft-boiled egg is marinated in a soy-based tare, giving it a savoury, slightly sweet flavour with a jammy yolk. The overall flavour profile is umami-rich, savoury, and slightly sweet, with a tender, melt-in-the-mouth texture from the braised pork belly contrasting with the firmer chashu slices. Nutritionally, this dish is high in protein (approximately 14.5 g per 100g) and fat (approximately 16.2 g per 100g), with low carbohydrate content (approximately 2.8 g per 100g), resulting in a low glycemic index of approximately 32. The sodium content is elevated at approximately 520 mg per 100g due to soy sauce marinades, making it a concern for individuals managing hypertension or cardiovascular disease. It is suitable for low-carbohydrate dietary patterns and is appropriate for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes in moderate portions. The dish is not halal due to pork content and contains egg and soy allergens.

confidence_reason: No direct nutritional entry exists for Ramen Matsuri Finger Bites in any approved primary source (HPB Singapore Foodo, USDA, ICMR-NIN, FAO/INFOODS). Nutrient values are estimated from a weighted composite of USDA SR Legacy entries for braised pork belly, pork/chicken chashu, and soft-boiled egg. GI is an analogous estimate from approved GI database sources. Confidence is medium rather than low because the component ingredients are well-characterised in approved sources and the estimation methodology is transparent and defensible.
notes: 1. Nutrient values are composite estimates from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entries for braised pork belly, chashu-style pork and chicken, and soft-boiled marinated egg; no direct approved-source entry exists for this specific restaurant dish. 2. Sodium value (520 mg per 100g) includes an upward adjustment for soy sauce and mirin marinade contribution typical of Japanese braised meat preparations. 3. dietary_fibre_g is 0.0 as this dish contains no plant-based fibre sources; confirmed zero based on ingredient composition (meat and egg only). 4. GI of 32 is an analogous estimate; no direct GI measurement for this dish exists in the University of Sydney GI Database. 5. monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, potassium_mg, calcium_mg, iron_mg, cholesterol_mg, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, and water_g are estimated from USDA SR Legacy component data and are not individually confirmed from a single source entry. 6. The dish contains pork and is not halal. 7. Input description contained a typo ('pork ad chicken charsiew') interpreted as 'pork and chicken charsiew'. 8. Japanese local name ラーメン松りフィンガーバイツ is a transliteration approximation; the restaurant name Matsuri (松り) may use different kanji in official branding. 9. food_group reclassified from 'Grains and staples' (input) to 'Meat and alternatives' as the dominant macronutrient and ingredient profile is protein and fat from meat and egg, not grains.

q1_food_identity.notes: food_name is the canonical English name as used by the restaurant. Japanese local name provided per G6/Q1 mandatory language rule for Japanese cuisine. food_group corrected from input 'Grains and staples' to 'Meat and alternatives' based on ingredient composition.

q2_core_macro_completeness_and_source_in.notes: All four core macros (energy_kcal 218.0, protein_g 14.5, fat_g 16.2, carbohydrate_g 2.8) are present, numeric, and plausible. Nutrient source is USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy with transparent composite estimation methodology documented.

q3_secondary_nutrient_completeness.notes: dietary_fibre_g (0.0, confirmed zero — meat and egg dish with no plant fibre), sodium_mg (520.0), saturated_fat_g (5.8), and sugar_g (1.9) are all present as numeric values and are plausible for this dish type.

q4_glycemic_completeness.notes: glycemic_index 32 (low, ≤55 threshold satisfied), gi_category 'low', glycemic_load_per_serving 1.3, gi_source documented with University of Sydney GI Database and Atkinson et al. 2008 peer-reviewed journal reference. Analogous estimate basis fully documented.

q5_ontological_alignment.notes: FoodOn closest_parent FOODON_03315595 (pork dish) and FoodEx2 closest_parent A00LC (Pork meat products) are defensible and honest. SNOMED CT is null with gap note. No codes are invented. match_type values are all from the permitted set: exact, closest_parent, none.

q6_llm_ready_semantics.notes: natural_language_description is well over 200 characters (approximately 1,450 characters), covers food identity, preparation method, taste and texture, cultural context, clinical relevance, and key nutrients. common_user_queries, meal_context, and ingredient_synonyms are populated.

Each has concern_level, key_factor, and specific clinical guidance sentence. human_condition_name 'Type 2 Diabetes' used as required.
